response:
# Review: Thistle & Hearth, Easton
⭐⭐⭐⭐ 4/5 Stars

I dragged my feet about coming here for three months. My partner wouldn't shut up about it, but any spot with a permanent line out the door usually just means someone made a good TikTok of their sourdough. We showed up last Tuesday soaked through, our golden retriever dripping mud on the sidewalk, fully intending to turn around and get takeout curry until the host stuck her head out and yelled "there's a two top free right now, c'mon in".

First thing you notice: none of the wood is fake distressed. It's just worn, from ten years of people setting down mugs and elbows. The radio plays 70s folk quiet enough you don't have to lean in to talk. Before we even got menus, she brought the dog a bowl of cold water and a thick slice of bacon, no fanfare.

Bread came warm, with butter that still had little flecks of grass in it. We had turnip soup that tasted exactly like standing over a stove on a rainy day, not the bland pureed glue most places serve for $14. My pan fried trout had skin so crisp it cracked when you tapped it with a fork, sitting on lentils that someone had actually bothered to season with garlic and thyme, not just boil and dump on a plate.

Now the bad parts, because every real review has bad parts:
- We waited 41 minutes for our mains. I counted.
- Our waitress forgot our glass of pinot noir twice.
- The chocolate salt tart was catastrophically bad. Someone absolutely knocked over the flaky salt shaker mid-prep. We took two bites and pushed it away.

Here's the thing, though. When we mentioned the tart? No awkward defence. No "that's how it's supposed to be". She just winced, picked up the plate, and said "oh that's garbage, I'm so sorry that went out". Five minutes later she brought over two warm honey shortbreads, knocked the whole dessert off the bill, and didn't make a big show of it. No manager came over. No discount coupon for next time. Just basic decency, which is apparently an endangered species in restaurants now.

This isn't the place you fly across the country to eat at. It won't be on any top 10 lists. There's no tasting menu, no edible flowers, no neon sign that says *EAT GOOD FOOD* above the bar. It's just people who show up, care more about soup than aesthetics, and apologise properly when they mess up.

4 stars. I'm withholding one because 40 minutes is still too long for trout. Also if I gave them 5 they'd get cocky. We're going back next Tuesday. Don't tell anyone about this place, the line is already long enough.
